meta.title: Hirokiwa
meta.description: A portfolio page featuring Hirokiwa's profile and selected projects.
profile.heading: Hirokiwa
profile.descriptionHtml: A developer based in Japan. I discovered digital making at university and decided to become a software engineer.<wbr> While working at an IT company, I also build apps and create digital content as personal projects.
contents.heading: Contents
grandring.title: Anywhere Grand Ring
grandring.description: An interactive map that lets you place the Grand Ring, the symbol of Expo 2025 Osaka, Kansai, anywhere on the map and compare its scale. You can compare it not only with the Expo site, but also with landmarks across Japan and around the world to feel the scale of the world's largest wooden structure.
grandring.trendTitle: Expo Osaka's Grand Ring amazes people worldwide with its overwhelming scale
grandring.trendDescription: Bigger than expected. Internet users have been stunned one after another as the Expo 2025 Osaka, Kansai Grand Ring appears online covering famous landmarks around the world.
grandring.interviewLink1: Creator of the viral site that lets people place the Expo symbol anywhere from the Tokyo Dome to Mount Everest summit interviewed by Yomiuri Shimbun Online
grandring.interviewLink2: What if the Expo 2025 Osaka, Kansai Grand Ring were placed around Tokushima Prefecture? - Tokushima Shimbun Digital
grandring.interviewLink3: What if the Expo Grand Ring stood inside Kyoto City? The map-based site Anywhere Grand Ring appears - Kyoto Shimbun Digital
grandring.featureLink1: Is the Expo Grand Ring the same size as Edo Castle? The viral web service Anywhere Grand Ring reveals a surprising fact - Yahoo! News
grandring.featureLink2: Too huge? Anywhere Grand Ring lets you move the Expo Grand Ring into your own neighborhood - ITmedia NEWS
grandring.featureLink3: Even AI gets this wrong: How many Tokyo Domes fit inside the Expo site Grand Ring? - Nikkei Cross Trend
aiBlog.imageAlt: AI Ojisan Daily Blog delivered in Kansai dialect
aiBlog.title: AI Ojisan Daily Blog
aiBlog.description: A diary of muttered thoughts written by AI Ojisan, an old-man persona powered by a large-scale Kansai dialect model. The blog is updated every day at 7 PM (Japan time) with inhuman consistency.
aiBlog.bannerAlt: Official X banner for AI Ojisan
aiBlog.relatedLink1: I want to run a blog using generative AI alone #gpt - Qiita
qrReader.imageAlt: QR Reader via Copy and Paste
qrReader.title: QR Reader via Copy and Paste
qrReader.mainLinkLabel: QR Reader via Copy and Paste
qrReader.description: A QR code reader for desktop use. It reads a QR code image copied to your clipboard with a single click, removing the hassle of opening your smartphone camera just to scan a code shown on your computer screen.
